candidates.headline: Full P&L ownership across a $1.8B revenue base with direct PE board reporting. Consolidated 28 manufacturing sites to 22 while increasing output 14%.
candidates.career_trajectory: Plant Mgr → VP Ops → SVP (PE portfolio)

scope_match.candidate_actual: 22 sites across North America and Mexico
Three PE-backed roles since 2016, reports to operating partner
US, Mexico, Germany; JV in Guangzhou

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Marcus presents an unusually close scope match across all five dimensions — particularly notable is the direct PE board reporting cadence and the footprint consolidation experience.

key_criteria.name: P&L Ownership at Scale
key_criteria.evidence: Full P&L ownership across $1.8B revenue base spanning powertrain and chassis divisions with direct accountability to PE board for margin targets

key_criteria.name: Multi-Site Manufacturing Leadership
key_criteria.evidence: Consolidated 28 manufacturing sites to 22 while simultaneously increasing output by 14% through regional centers of excellence

key_criteria.name: Continuous Improvement & Footprint Optimization
key_criteria.evidence: Deployed Lean Six Sigma enterprise-wide, closing 6 underperforming plants and delivering $47M in annual savings through footprint rationalization

key_criteria.name: Private Equity Operating Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Three PE-backed operating roles since 2016 — fluent in value creation plans, 100-day planning, and quarterly PE board cadence

key_criteria.name: Global Operations Scope
key_criteria.evidence: Managed operations across US, Mexico, and Germany and established a joint venture manufacturing facility in Guangzhou

compensation.flexibility: Flexible on structure. Primary driver is equity participation in the new platform.

why_interested.headline: Full P&L ownership at group level — not just an operational slice
why_interested.detail: Currently SVP Ops but reports into a CEO. Wants the top operational seat where he owns the entire value chain.

why_interested.headline: Transformation mandate — building, not maintaining
why_interested.detail: Drawn to the idea of leading a full manufacturing transformation rather than inheriting a playbook.

why_interested.headline: PE exit timeline creates 18-month window
why_interested.detail: Current PE sponsor approaching exit. Proactively exploring before the transaction closes his options.

our_take.text: Marcus is the strongest match in this shortlist. His operational depth, PE fluency, and demonstrated ability to consolidate manufacturing footprints while growing output directly mirrors the mandate. The only gap — no direct commercial/sales experience — is manageable for a COO role where operational transformation is the primary lever. We recommend advancing with confidence.
our_take_fragments: Closest scope match we've seen — $1.8B P&L, 22 sites, PE board cadence
Footprint consolidation is his signature — 28 to 22 plants, $47M savings
No commercial experience is the one gap — but COO remit is operations-first
PE exit timing means he's available and motivated

edc_data.motivation_hook: Full transformation ownership before PE exit closes the window

candidates.headline: Oversees 18 manufacturing plants across North America and Europe. Leading the strategic ICE-to-EV footprint transition currently in execution.

scope_match.candidate_actual: $950M operational budget (indirect P&L influence over $1.4B division)
18 plants across North America and Europe
Public company; led one carve-out as operational lead

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Sarah's manufacturing depth is exceptional, but P&L ownership is indirect and PE cadence is limited. The step-up to full COO authority is the key question.

key_criteria.name: P&L Ownership at Scale
key_criteria.evidence: Managed $950M operational budget contributing to 340bps EBITDA improvement over 3 years — but without standalone P&L authority

key_criteria.name: Multi-Site Manufacturing Leadership
key_criteria.evidence: Oversees 18 plants across North America and Europe with standardized operating model and two new EV-specific facilities in build

key_criteria.name: Continuous Improvement & Footprint Optimization
key_criteria.evidence: Led Toyota Production System deployment achieving 23% scrap reduction and 31% OEE improvement across legacy ICE operations

key_criteria.name: Private Equity Operating Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Limited direct PE experience — one carve-out transaction as operational lead when Meridian divested its aftermarket division

key_criteria.name: Global Operations Scope
key_criteria.evidence: Integrated two acquired Hungarian plants post-acquisition within 14 months; functional German language skills

compensation.flexibility: Flexible on base if equity component is meaningful. Willing to discuss earnout structures.

why_interested.headline: Step-up to full COO scope including supply chain and procurement
why_interested.detail: Wants authority beyond manufacturing — supply chain, procurement, and facilities.

why_interested.headline: PE environment where decisions get made quickly
why_interested.detail: Attracted to PE decisioning speed versus public company consensus culture.

why_interested.headline: Feels boxed into the manufacturing lane at current employer
why_interested.detail: Despite VP title, scope is limited to plant operations only.

our_take.text: Sarah brings exceptional manufacturing depth and a transformation track record — particularly the ICE-to-EV footprint transition. The gap is full P&L ownership and PE cadence, but she is articulate about why she wants the step-up. Worth advancing to test whether the PE gap is real or quickly learnable.
our_take_fragments: Manufacturing depth is best-in-class — 18 sites, TPS deployment, EV transition
P&L is indirect — budget accountability, not standalone profit and loss
PE cadence is the biggest gap — only one carve-out, no board reporting rhythm
Hungry for the step-up and articulate about why

edc_data.motivation_hook: Full COO authority beyond the manufacturing lane

candidates.headline: Current COO with full P&L at $420M — strong PE operating cadence but significant scale gap versus the $2B mandate.

scope_match.candidate_actual: Current role PE-backed (Greenfield Capital), QBR cadence, one bolt-on
Primarily US domestic; one facility in Monterrey, Mexico

edc_data.scope_seasoning: David has the right operating instincts and PE muscle, but the scale gap ($420M vs $2B) and limited global footprint are the key questions for discussion.

key_criteria.name: P&L Ownership at Scale
key_criteria.evidence: Full P&L ownership at $420M revenue — genuine COO authority but roughly one-fifth of the $2B target scale

key_criteria.name: Multi-Site Manufacturing Leadership
key_criteria.evidence: Oversees 8 manufacturing sites; prior role included 15 sites — combined experience of 23 but never simultaneously at the 25-site scale

key_criteria.name: Continuous Improvement & Footprint Optimization
key_criteria.evidence: Implemented operational excellence framework delivering 18% cost reduction — but has not led a major footprint rationalization or plant closure program

key_criteria.name: Private Equity Operating Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Current role is PE-backed with quarterly business review cadence and direct involvement in one bolt-on acquisition achieving synergy targets 4 months early

key_criteria.name: Global Operations Scope
key_criteria.evidence: Primarily domestic US operations with one facility in Monterrey, Mexico — no European or Asian operational experience

compensation.flexibility: Compensation expectations are well within range. Motivated primarily by scale and sector return, not comp.

why_interested.headline: Return to automotive at genuine scale
why_interested.detail: Left automotive five years ago for the COO title. Now has the title and PE experience, wants to apply both at automotive scale.

why_interested.headline: Describes the move as 'coming home at a higher altitude'
why_interested.detail: Self-aware about the sector gap and framing the return as a deliberate career arc.

why_interested.headline: Outgrown the current platform's scale ceiling
why_interested.detail: $420M is the maximum Great Lakes will reach. Wants a larger canvas.

our_take.text: David has the right instincts and PE operating cadence, but the scale and global gaps are real. His $420M P&L is a meaningful step down from the $2B mandate, and limited international exposure is a concern for 25-site global oversight. Worth a conversation if the client values PE fluency and cultural fit — but should not be positioned as the lead candidate.
our_take_fragments: PE operating muscle is genuine — QBR cadence, bolt-on integration, profit interest
Scale gap is the headline risk — $420M is one-fifth of the mandate
Five years out of automotive — sector re-entry at COO level carries adaptation risk
Comp expectations are well within range — motivated by scale, not money

edc_data.motivation_hook: Coming home to automotive at a higher altitude

candidates.headline: Full operational turnaround of $680M PE-backed industrial manufacturer — consolidated 24 plants to 18, expanded EBITDA margin from 9% to 17% in four years. Previously SVP Operations at Roper Technologies running $1.2B P&L across 22 sites.
candidates.career_trajectory: Plant Mgr → SVP Ops (Roper $1.2B) → CEO (PE portfolio)

scope_match.candidate_actual: CEO of Blackford Capital portco, 4 years

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Hargrove has run this exact playbook — PE-backed turnaround, plant consolidation, margin expansion — at slightly smaller scale. The step from $680M CEO to $2B COO is a scale increase but not a capability gap. His Roper experience at $1.2B bridges the delta. Strongest operational match in the search.

key_criteria.name: P&L Ownership at Scale
key_criteria.evidence: Full P&L ownership at Roper Technologies ($1.2B) across industrial instrumentation division; at Pryor, runs entire P&L at $680M with board-level accountability to PE sponsor

key_criteria.name: Multi-Site Manufacturing Leadership
key_criteria.evidence: 22 plants at Roper across US, UK, and Germany; currently 18 sites at Pryor post-consolidation from 24 — deep experience managing geographically dispersed operations

key_criteria.name: Continuous Improvement & Footprint Optimization
key_criteria.evidence: Built Pryor's operational excellence programme from zero — Six Sigma Black Belt, reduced scrap rates by 34% and improved OEE from 62% to 81% in 30 months

key_criteria.name: Private Equity Operating Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Four years as CEO of Blackford Capital portfolio company — managed quarterly board reporting, 100-day plan execution, and integrated 2 bolt-on acquisitions

key_criteria.name: Global Operations Scope
key_criteria.evidence: Led Pryor's plant consolidation: 24 sites to 18 with $28M in annualised cost savings; at Roper, integrated 3 acquisitions into existing manufacturing footprint without adding facilities

compensation.expected_total: Comparable base + equity package — equity component is the negotiation point
compensation.flexibility: Compensation expectations at upper end of range — equity component will be the negotiation point.

why_interested.headline: Larger platform as COO rather than CEO — 'I want to run operations at scale, not manage investor relations'
why_interested.detail: Wants to focus on operations at $2B scale rather than CEO-level investor relations and board management.

why_interested.headline: Detroit is home — $2B automotive scale is the step up he's looking for
why_interested.detail: Originally from Metro Detroit. Sees this as the culmination of his operational career arc.

why_interested.headline: Pryor approaching PE exit window — proactively exploring
why_interested.detail: Currently in Year 4 of Blackford Capital hold period. Approaching typical exit timeline and wants to secure next platform before transition.

our_take.text: Best operator we've seen in this search. Quiet authority — the kind of person who walks a plant floor and the supervisors straighten up. PE-fluent without being PE-dependent. Turned Pryor around without a single site closure that made the news, which tells you something about how he manages people through change. Only question is whether he'd step back from CEO to COO — but his interest is real. Would absolutely hold a board room. First choice for shortlist.
our_take_fragments: Best operator in the search — quiet authority, PE-fluent, transformation track record
Turned Pryor around: $420M→$680M revenue, EBITDA 9%→17%, 24→18 sites, $28M savings
Only question is CEO→COO step-back — but his interest is genuine and well-articulated
Would hold a board room — first choice for shortlist

edc_data.motivation_hook: Scale step-up from CEO to COO at larger platform, post-exit timing

candidates.current_title: Vice President, North American Manufacturing Operations
candidates.headline: 16-year Vanguard veteran overseeing 19 North American plants with $1.8B operational P&L and 8,200 employees. Deep automotive expertise but single-company tenure, zero PE experience, and non-compete concerns place him on hold.
candidates.career_trajectory: Manufacturing Eng → Plant Dir → VP NA Manufacturing (16 years at Vanguard)

edc_data.current_title: Vice President, North American Manufacturing Operations

scope_match.candidate_actual: $1.8B NA operations P&L, reports to Group COO
19 plants across US, Canada, and Mexico

edc_data.scope_seasoning: DeLuca has the scale and the sector but has never operated outside one company or one ownership structure. The P&L is right, the plants are nearly right, but PE experience is absent and his entire career is Vanguard. The question is whether 16 years of depth compensates for zero breadth.

key_criteria.name: P&L Ownership at Scale
key_criteria.evidence: Runs North American operations P&L of approximately $1.8B with full budget authority, reporting directly to Group COO — figure based on candidate's characterisation of 'just over half of group revenue'

key_criteria.name: Multi-Site Manufacturing Leadership
key_criteria.evidence: 19 plants across US, Canada, and Mexico — managed expansion from 14 to 19 sites during EV transition buildout and standardised production systems across all facilities

key_criteria.name: Continuous Improvement & Footprint Optimization
key_criteria.evidence: Led Vanguard's Precision Manufacturing System reducing warranty claims by 41% over 3 years — however, system was inherited, not built; DeLuca optimised an existing framework

key_criteria.name: Private Equity Operating Experience
key_criteria.evidence: None — 16 years at a publicly listed company with no exposure to PE board dynamics, value creation plans, or compressed timelines

key_criteria.name: Global Operations Scope
key_criteria.evidence: Added 5 new plants during EV transition — experience is in expansion, not consolidation; no track record of plant closures, rationalisation, or M&A integration

compensation.flexibility: Expects equity component. Comp expectations within range — not the negotiation issue.

why_interested.headline: PE environment for 'faster decision-making and clearer accountability'
why_interested.detail: Attracted to PE decisioning speed versus public company consensus culture.

why_interested.headline: Passed over for Group COO — role went to an external hire last year
why_interested.detail: Feels he has hit the ceiling at Vanguard. Group COO role went to an external hire, which he took as a signal.

why_interested.headline: Detroit-based, no relocation needed — seeks step-up with PE exposure
why_interested.detail: Lives in Dearborn, 15 minutes from the client. No relocation friction.

our_take.text: On paper he's the obvious choice — right sector, right scale, right geography, and he's 15 minutes down the road. But three things give us pause. One: he's never left Vanguard, and 16 years at one company means we don't know how he adapts. Two: zero PE experience, and this is a PE-backed role where the board expects a certain cadence. Three: the non-compete is a real issue — Vanguard's legal team is aggressive on this and a direct competitor hire could get messy. Put him on hold until we've tested the frontrunners. If Hargrove falls through, DeLuca is the fallback conversation — but go in with eyes open.
our_take_fragments: Right sector, right scale, right geography — the obvious choice on paper
16 years at one company — untested adaptability is the headline risk
Zero PE experience — would need significant onboarding to PE cadence
Non-compete is a real issue — Vanguard's legal team is aggressive

edc_data.motivation_hook: Passed over for Group COO internally, seeking step-up with PE exposure

candidates.current_title: Regional Director of Operations, EMEA & Asia-Pacific
candidates.headline: Cross-border operations leader managing 9 plants across 5 countries with €640M operational budget. Toyota Production System trained (TSSC Japan). Led post-acquisition integration of two French facilities. Open to US relocation — family ties in Chicago.
candidates.career_trajectory: Industrial Eng → Plant Dir → Regional Dir Ops EMEA/APAC

edc_data.current_title: Regional Director of Operations, EMEA & Asia-Pacific

scope_match.candidate_actual: 9 plants across 5 countries (DE, FR, UK, IN, MY)
3 years under Cinven ownership, bolt-on DD

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Wendt operates at roughly 35% of the required revenue scale and 36% of the site count — but the complexity per site is arguably higher than the target role. Managing across 5 countries with 5 different regulatory and labour environments, in aerospace precision (tighter tolerances than automotive), with TPS credentials, is a different kind of readiness.

key_criteria.name: P&L Ownership at Scale
key_criteria.evidence: Operational P&L of €640M (~$700M) across EMEA and APAC — does not own full divisional P&L but step up from current scale is significant

key_criteria.name: Multi-Site Manufacturing Leadership
key_criteria.evidence: 9 plants across 5 countries — below threshold but complexity is high, managing across German, French, British, Indian, and Malaysian operations with different labour laws and union structures

key_criteria.name: Continuous Improvement & Footprint Optimization
key_criteria.evidence: Toyota Production System trained (3-month TSSC secondment, Japan) — deployed TPS-derived lean system across all 9 plants, reduced cycle times 28% and improved first-pass yield from 91% to 97.3%

key_criteria.name: Private Equity Operating Experience
key_criteria.evidence: 3 years under Cinven ownership — participated in 100-day plan, managed quarterly board presentations, led operational due diligence for one bolt-on acquisition

key_criteria.name: Global Operations Scope
key_criteria.evidence: Integrated 2 French plants post-acquisition (Aérotech Précision, 2023) — consolidated overlapping production lines, relocated one product line from Toulouse to Stuttgart

compensation.expected_total: US market rate + relocation support (visa, housing, family transition)
compensation.flexibility: Open on structure. Relocation support is the key requirement beyond base compensation.

edc_data.notice_period: 6 months (standard German executive contract — potential to negotiate to 3 months)

why_interested.headline: US relocation window — wants experience before children start secondary school
why_interested.detail: Timing window is now. Family ties in Chicago. Sees this as a once-in-a-career opportunity.

why_interested.headline: Scale step-up from regional to group-level operations
why_interested.detail: 'I've done the multi-country complexity. Now I want the scale to match.'

why_interested.headline: Regional director ceiling — Group COO seat at Breitenfeld is occupied
why_interested.detail: No clear path to group-level operations at current employer.

our_take.text: Interesting profile. The Toyota pedigree is real — you don't get a TSSC secondment without being exceptional, and the first-pass yield numbers back it up. Cross-cultural plant management is a genuine differentiator that none of the other candidates offer. The gaps are scale (9 plants not 15, €640M not $1B+) and she's never worked in the US. But she's PE-literate, lean-credentialed, and the aerospace precision background means she won't be fazed by automotive tolerances. Relocation is a real question — says she's open, has family in Chicago, but Stuttgart to Detroit is a life change. Worth a first interview to test the fit. Submitted as a wildcard — if the search wants someone who brings international operational depth, she's the one.
our_take_fragments: Toyota pedigree is real — TSSC secondment, first-pass yield 91%→97.3%
Cross-cultural plant management is a differentiator none of the others offer
Scale gap is real — 9 plants and €640M is 35% of the target
Wildcard submission — if the search values international operational depth, she's the one

edc_data.motivation_hook: US relocation window, scale step-up from regional to group-level operations

candidates.headline: Accomplished FMCG operations leader with $1.4B P&L across 12 plants — strong general management and PE credentials but career entirely in consumer goods. No automotive, industrial, or precision manufacturing exposure. Sector mismatch.
candidates.career_trajectory: Ops Analyst → Plant Dir → VP Ops → SVP Global Ops (PE-backed FMCG)

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Callahan has PE depth and respectable scale, but his entire career is consumer goods — personal care, household products, retail supply chains. Automotive manufacturing operates on different quality systems (IATF 16949 not ISO 22716), different customer dynamics, and different capex cycles. The sector gap is too wide.

key_criteria.name: P&L Ownership at Scale
key_criteria.evidence: Full operational P&L of $1.4B across global manufacturing — meets the scale requirement but FMCG P&L dynamics are structurally different from automotive

key_criteria.name: Multi-Site Manufacturing Leadership
key_criteria.evidence: 12 plants in US and Latin America — below threshold and plants are high-volume batch/continuous process (liquids, aerosols), not discrete manufacturing

key_criteria.name: Continuous Improvement & Footprint Optimization
key_criteria.evidence: Led Lakeshore Performance System across all 12 sites — TPM, SMED, and 5S deployment, but continuous improvement in FMCG is a different discipline from automotive (APQP, PPAP, SPC absent)

key_criteria.name: Private Equity Operating Experience
key_criteria.evidence: 5 years under Advent International ownership — led 2 bolt-on acquisition integrations, manages quarterly board reporting; strongest area relative to the brief

key_criteria.name: Global Operations Scope
key_criteria.evidence: Consolidated 3 plants post-acquisition into existing Lakeshore facilities — effective execution but in FMCG context where consolidation decisions differ fundamentally from automotive

compensation.flexibility: Comp expectations within range. Not the issue — sector mismatch is the issue.

why_interested.headline: Wants to move from FMCG to industrial — 'I want to build things that matter'
why_interested.detail: Feels consumer goods operations are becoming commoditised. Attracted to automotive complexity and capex intensity.

why_interested.headline: Feels FMCG operations becoming commoditised
why_interested.detail: Consumer goods manufacturing increasingly outsourced to co-packers. Wants to run real manufacturing.

why_interested.headline: Attracted to automotive complexity and capex intensity
why_interested.detail: Self-aware about the sector gap but believes operational fundamentals transfer.

our_take.text: Good operator, wrong sector. The PE experience is strong and the scale is close, but everything else is consumer goods — personal care, household products, retail supply chains. Automotive manufacturing is a different animal: different quality systems, different customer dynamics, different capex cycles. Callahan would spend his first year learning the sector rather than transforming operations. Plenty of automotive COO candidates available — no reason to cross-sector here. Declined.
our_take_fragments: PE experience is strong — 5 years under Advent, 2 bolt-on integrations
Everything else is consumer goods — wrong quality systems, wrong customer dynamics
Would spend Year 1 learning the sector rather than transforming operations
Plenty of automotive COO candidates available — no reason to cross-sector

edc_data.motivation_hook: Seeking sector transition from FMCG to industrial manufacturing
